big_picture: Global market volatility is driving investor caution, with NSE’s Harish Ahuja advising retail investors to "Don’t panic, stay invested." The US has lifted sanctions on Iranian oil at sea to ease supply pressures, prompting refiners in Asia to consider buying Iranian oil. Meanwhile, Iran reportedly fired two missiles at Diego Garcia military base in the Indian Ocean, though neither hit. A new Reserve Bank notification, Foreign Exchange Management (Export and Import of Currency) (Amendment) Regulations, 2026, has also been issued.

Additionally, India is reviewing the legal implications of a potential India-US trade deal tied to new US tariff policies.

articles.title: Donald Trump ‘very surprised’ Australia declined to send troops to strait of Hormuz amid fuel crisis
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump expressed surprise that Australia declined to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz amid a blockade affecting global oil supply and fuel prices. Trump stated he had expected Australia to assist, despite earlier comments that the US did not need help from Australia, Japan, or South Korea. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical route for global oil shipments, and its blockade has raised concerns about energy security and fuel price volatility worldwide.

articles.title: ITAT Allows 10% Safe Harbour on DVO Valuation Due to Estimation Nature of Property Values
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a 10% safe harbour limit applies to property valuations determined by the District Valuation Officer (DVO), not just stamp duty values. The tribunal favored the taxpayer (assessee) as the valuation variation was within the 10% threshold. This decision clarifies tax compliance rules for property valuation disputes in India.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal held that the safe harbour limit applies to valuation determined by the DVO, not just stamp duty value. articles.title: Procedural Lapse Cannot Override Substantive Claim: ITAT Allows Depreciation Correction
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that procedural errors in tax appeals cannot override substantive tax claims. The tribunal dismissed an appeal because the actual tax effect was below the Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) threshold, emphasizing strict adherence to monetary limits for appeals. The decision reinforces the importance of correct tax calculations and procedural compliance in tax disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that the maintainability of the appeal must be based on the correct tax effect and not erroneous figures in Form 36. Since the actual tax effect was below the CBDT threshold, the appeal was dismissed, reinforcing strict adherence to monetary limits. The post Procedural Lapse Cannot Override Substantive Claim: ITAT Allows Depreciation Correction appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Legislative Gaps, Judicial Grafts: The IRC Transition

articles.summary: The court declined to interfere with reassessment proceedings for a deceased assessee's estate, stating that factual disputes must be addressed through statutory appeals rather than writ jurisdiction. articles.title: Global markets to remain driven by West Asia conflict, Fed neutral guidance reflects uncertainty: Report

articles.title: US taps emergency oil reserve, releases 45 million barrels to tame war-driven price hike
articles.summary: The US Trump administration is releasing 45.2 million barrels from its Strategic Petroleum Reserve to stabilize soaring oil prices amid the ongoing war with Iran. The move involves lending crude to energy firms with repayment terms, aiming to curb price hikes without taxpayer cost. This global effort seeks to stabilize energy markets amid geopolitical tensions. The reserve is expected to grow back upon repayment.
articles.raw_summary: Amidst the ongoing war with Iran, the Trump administration is releasing 45.2 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve to curb soaring oil prices. This move, part of a global effort, involves lending crude to energy firms with repayment including a premium, aiming to stabilize markets without taxpayer cost. The reserve, holding significant oil, is expected to grow upon repayment.

articles.title: Travel to laundry: Companiess pass on price hikes, budgets to get hit
articles.summary: Companies are passing on price hikes to consumers, straining household budgets and raising fears of job losses in labour-heavy industries. The article highlights concerns over inflation and its economic impact, particularly in sectors reliant on manual labour. No direct policy action or demand is mentioned, but the situation could prompt government intervention.
articles.raw_summary: Price Of War: Job-Loss Fears Grow In Labour-Intensive Sectors

articles.title: 'Bring almost 140 mn barrels to global markets': After Russian, US waives sanctions on Iranian oil
articles.summary: The US has granted a 30-day waiver on Iranian oil shipments currently at sea, aiming to stabilize global energy supplies amid escalating Middle East tensions. This move is intended to inject approximately 140 million barrels into the market. The US asserts control over revenue, while Iran claims no surplus oil is available. The decision follows similar relaxations on Russian oil.
articles.raw_summary: The US has granted a 30-day waiver on Iranian oil shipments currently at sea, aiming to stabilize global energy supplies amid escalating Middle East tensions. This move, intended to inject approximately 140 million barrels into the market, follows similar relaxations on Russian oil. While the US asserts control over revenue, Iran claims no surplus oil is available.

articles.title: New I-T Act framework notified; taxpayer relief with stricter compliance from April 1
articles.summary: The Income Tax Act has introduced new rules effective April 1, offering relief to taxpayers through increased house rent allowance limits in major cities and expanded employer-provided benefits like food coupons and cars. However, stricter disclosure requirements have been imposed on businesses and professionals, increasing compliance responsibilities for auditors and companies.
articles.raw_summary: New Income Tax Act rules are now in effect from April 1. These rules offer relief to taxpayers with increased house rent allowance limits in major cities. Employer-provided benefits like food coupons and cars are also expanded. Stricter disclosure requirements are introduced for businesses and professionals. Auditors and companies face greater responsibility in this compliance-driven regime.

articles.title: Why the rupee crossed 93 against dollar: Key drivers explained
articles.summary: The Indian rupee fell past 93 per US dollar for the first time on March 20, hitting 93.49. Factors include West Asia tensions, rising crude oil prices, a stronger US dollar, and foreign fund outflows.
articles.raw_summary: The Indian rupee fell past 93 per US dollar for the first time on March 20, hitting 93.49. Factors include West Asia tensions, rising crude oil prices, a stronger US dollar, and foreign fund outflows.

articles.title: TMC unveils 10-point manifesto for West Bengal polls, pledges welfare expansion and healthcare outreach
articles.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee unveiled the Trinamool Congress (TMC) party's 10-point manifesto ahead of state elections, pledging to expand welfare programs and healthcare outreach. The manifesto serves as a roadmap for the government if TMC returns to power for a fourth term. The commitments include welfare and healthcare initiatives, signaling potential policy priorities for the state. Banerjee stated the manifesto would guide the government's actions if re-elected.
articles.raw_summary: Unveiling the manifesto, Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee said the commitments would serve as a guiding roadmap for the government if the party returns to power for a fourth consecutive term.

The announcement highlights the party's preparations for the state elections, which could influence future governance and policy directions in Assam.

articles.title: No Congress MP to contest Kerala Assembly polls, party banks on state leaders
articles.summary: The Congress party has decided not to field any Members of Parliament (MPs) in the upcoming Kerala Assembly elections scheduled for April 9. Instead, the party will rely on its state-level leaders to contest the 92 seats. Kannur MP K Sudhakaran, who initially expressed dissatisfaction, has agreed to comply with the decision. The party has finalized candidates for all seats.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that officials will be held accountable for their inaction in preventing illegal sand mining in the Chambal Sanctuary, which has led to wildlife destruction. The Court emphasized that lethargy and failure to act will result in liability. This decision underscores the judiciary's role in enforcing environmental protection laws and could influence future policy on sand mining and wildlife conservation.

His new term begins amid rising domestic challenges, including the economic impact of surging oil prices caused by the conflict in Iran. The situation may influence Thailand's energy policies and regional trade, which could indirectly affect India's energy security and economic strategies.
articles.raw_summary: The 59-year-old now faces the challenge of dealing with the domestic impacts of the oil price shock triggered by the war in Iran.

articles.title: CERC Approves Tariffs for NHPC’s 1.2 GW Wind-Solar Hybrid Power Projects
articles.summary: The Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (CERC) has approved tariffs of ₹3.41 (~$0.036)/kWh and ₹3.42 (~$0.037)/kWh for NHPC’s 1.2 GW wind-solar hybrid power projects. The approval includes power purchase agreements (PPAs) and ensures alignment with Ministry of Power guidelines. This decision supports India’s renewable energy goals by facilitating large-scale clean power projects.
articles.raw_summary: The Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (CERC) has approved tariffs of ₹3.41 (~$0.036)/kWh and ₹3.42 (~$0.037)/kWh for NHPC’s 1.2 GW of interstate transmission system (ISTS)-connected wind-solar hybrid power projects with a greenshoe option. It also approved the power purchase agreements (PPAs) and clarified that the trading margin and implementation structure would ensure alignment with the Ministry of Power guidelines.

articles.title: Gazette notification may allow more ministries to block content
articles.summary: The government may use a gazette notification to grant multiple ministries the power to block online content under the Information Technology Act, 2000, without requiring legal amendments. Secretaries of these ministries would act as 'nodal officers' to issue such orders, expanding Section 69(A) of the Act. This move could significantly broaden the scope of content regulation in India.
articles.raw_summary: Sources told us that the government could use a gazette notification to give multiple ministries content-blocking powers under the Information Technology Act, 2000, avoiding the need for legal amendments. This would make Secretaries 'nodal officers' with authority to issue orders, expanding Section 69(A) without facing legal hurdles.

articles.title: India-China border trade via Lipulekh Pass to resume after six years

articles.title: Rate of interest on Government of India Floating Rate Bond 2033
articles.summary: The rate of interest on Government of India Floating Rate Bond 2033 (FRB 2033) applicable for the half year March 22, 2026 to September 21, 2026 shall be 6.75 percent per annum. It may be recalled that FRB 2033 carries a coupon, which has a base rate equivalent to the average of the Weighted Average Yield (WAY) of last three auctions (from the rate fixing day, i.e., March 22, 2026) of 182 Day T-Bills, plus a fixed spread (1.22 percent). This initiative reflects the central government's focus on improving experiential learning in public sector skill development.

articles.title: National Biodiversity Authority constitutes Expert Committee on Invasive Alien Species
articles.summary: The National Biodiversity Authority (NBA) has formed an Expert Committee on Invasive Alien Species. This committee will advise on managing non-native species that threaten India's ecosystems. The move aims to strengthen biodiversity conservation efforts under India's legal framework. The NBA is a statutory body under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.

articles.summary: The Department of Telecommunications (DoT) has announced the 5G Innovation Hackathon 2026 as part of the 100 5G Use Case Labs Initiative. This initiative aims to foster innovation in 5G technology by inviting participants to develop practical use cases. The DoT, under the Ministry of Communications, is leading this effort to accelerate India's adoption of 5G services.

articles.title: MP High Court Issues Notice On Plea Alleging Flaws In Driving License Issuance Process, Road Safety Failure
articles.summary: The Madhya Pradesh High Court has issued notice on a petition alleging systemic failures in road safety, including alleged irregularities in issuance of driving licenses, faulty road construction and lack of accountability among authorities.� The petition states that the RTO issues hundreds of driving licenses each day. Assuming that each test takes approximately 15 minutes, the petition states that issuing such a high number of licenses within a single day would be 'mathematically impossible'.

The case involves Gabbar Singh alias Devendra Pratap Singh alias Rajesh Singh versus the State of U.P. and others. The ruling emphasizes the need for adherence to legal procedures to prevent misuse of the Act. The judgment underscores the judiciary's role in ensuring due process in law enforcement.

articles.title: UP Ganster Act Has Perilious Consequences, So Strict Compliance With Procedure Mandatory : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that strict compliance with procedure is mandatory under the UP Gangster Act after quashing an FIR for procedural irregularities. The Court emphasized that non-compliance with legal procedures can have perilous consequences. This ruling underscores the importance of due process in law enforcement under the Act.
articles.raw_summary: The Court quashed an FIR under the UP Gangster Act for procedural irregularities.

articles.title: Criminals Started Coming Into Legal Profession, BCI Tells Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court asked the Bar Council of India (BCI) what actions it has taken against lawyers who commit crimes after joining the legal profession. articles.title: HCs Can Look Beyond FIR/Complaint To Quash Frivolous Or Vexatious Criminal Proceedings : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that High Courts can look beyond the FIR or complaint to quash criminal proceedings if they find them frivolous or vexatious. The judgment allows courts to consider surrounding circumstances when deciding to dismiss such cases. This decision impacts how courts handle false or malicious criminal complaints in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Courts can consider attendant circumstances to quash proceedings based on frivolous or vexatious complaints.

articles.title: Section 60(4) of Social Security Code putting age limit for adoptive mothers to avail maternity benefit is unconstitutional
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that Section 60(4) of the Social Security Code, 2020, which imposed a three-month age limit on adopted children for adoptive mothers to avail maternity benefits, is unconstitutional. The court held that this provision violated Articles 14 (equality) and 21 (right to life and personal liberty) of the Constitution. The petition challenging the law was allowed, and the section was struck down.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court held that section 60(4) of the Social Security Code, 2020 putting an age limit of three months on the age of the adoptive child, for the adoptive mothers to avail maternity benefit is violative of Articles 14, and 21 of the Constitution respectively. Accordingly, the petition stands allowed. articles.summary: Three weeks into a Middle East war, Iran threatened to expand its retaliatory attacks to include global tourism and recreational sites. The U.S. responded by deploying additional warships and Marines to the region. The escalation raises concerns about regional stability and potential spillover effects that could impact India's security and diplomatic interests.
articles.raw_summary: Three weeks into an escalating war in the Middle East, Iran threatened Friday to expand its retaliatory attacks to include recreational and tourist sites worldwide, as the U.S. announced it was sending more warships and Marines to the region.

articles.title: Israeli and Iranian strikes on oil and gas facilities rattle global markets
articles.summary: Israeli and Iranian strikes on oil and gas facilities in the Persian Gulf have disrupted petroleum infrastructure, raising concerns about global energy supply. Susan Ziadeh, a former U.S. ambassador to Qatar, discusses the impact on Gulf countries and global markets. The strikes highlight regional instability with potential ripple effects on energy prices and India's import-dependent economy.
articles.raw_summary: To discuss the number of petroleum facilities that have been struck, and how the countries in the Persian Gulf view the war in Iran, Geoff Bennett spoke with Susan Ziadeh. She served as U.S. ambassador to Qatar during the Obama administration and is now a senior adviser at the Center for Strategic and International Studies.

articles.title: Japan's prime minister emerges buoyed from Trump summit
articles.summary: Japan's Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi met with former US President Donald Trump, focusing on trade, investment, and deeper economic cooperation rather than military support in the Middle East. The summit highlighted potential shifts in Japan-US defence and economic policies, which could influence India's strategic and trade relations with both nations.
articles.raw_summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi was wary that Trump might repeat his call for Japanese military support in the Middle East. The summit instead focused on trade, investment and deeper economic cooperation.

articles.title: Nuclear's cleanup cost threatens the expansion dream
articles.summary: Countries are rapidly expanding nuclear energy programs, but the high costs and unresolved challenges of cleaning up old nuclear reactors pose a major obstacle. The financial burden of decommissioning nuclear sites could derail expansion plans, raising concerns about long-term sustainability and safety. The issue underscores the need for robust policy frameworks to manage nuclear waste and ensure safe decommissioning.
articles.raw_summary: Countries are racing to build new reactors. But we've barely figured out how to clean up the old ones — and the bill is potentially staggering.

articles.title: Iran war: Why is the South Pars gas field so important?
articles.summary: An attack on Iran's South Pars gas field, a major global energy source, has disrupted energy markets and driven oil prices higher. The incident raises concerns about potential escalation in the region and its impact on global fuel supplies. India, as a major oil importer, may face implications for energy costs and policy decisions related to fuel subsidies or import diversification.
articles.raw_summary: An attack on Iran's key South Pars gas field has rattled global energy markets, raised fears of further escalation and driven oil prices even higher.

articles.title: Finland’s Stubb Cautions NATO Allies to Heed Trump’s Hormuz Call
articles.summary: Finland's President Alexander Stubb urged NATO allies to take seriously Donald Trump's call to protect the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil shipping route. articles.title: MCX gold falls over  ₹12,700/10g, silver down  ₹29,600/kg this week. Is safe-haven demand fading?
articles.summary: Gold and silver prices on the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX) have fallen sharply this week, with gold down over ₹12,700 per 10 grams and silver down ₹29,600 per kilogram. The decline is attributed to rising crude oil prices fueling inflation concerns and cautious central bank policies. Gold has dropped by ₹15,000 this month, while silver has fallen by nearly ₹29,645. The trend raises questions about whether safe-haven demand for these metals is weakening.
articles.raw_summary: Gold and silver prices on MCX are poised for their biggest weekly drop in over a month, driven by inflation fears from rising crude oil prices and cautious central bank policies. Gold has fallen by ₹15,000 this month, while silver has decreased by nearly ₹29,645.

articles.title: US takes down ‘cybercrime-as-a-service’ botnets that hijacked over 3 million IoT devices globally
articles.summary: The US Justice Department dismantled four major botnets that hijacked over 3 million IoT devices worldwide, using them to launch cyberattacks. These botnets operated as 'cybercrime-as-a-service,' enabling criminals to extort victims. The operation underscores the global scale of IoT-based cyber threats, which pose risks to critical infrastructure and data security. India may need to strengthen its cybersecurity policies and IoT regulations in response to such transnational threats.
articles.raw_summary: The US Justice Department has dismantled four major botnets responsible for significant DDoS attacks that infected millions of IoT devices globally. These botnets, operating on a 'cybercrime-as-a-service' model, were used by cybercriminals to launch attacks and extort victims for payments.

The incident highlights concerns over technology transfer restrictions and enforcement in the AI and semiconductor sectors.

articles.title: Whats(App) in a number? Usernames set to enter chat; global rollout likely in June
articles.summary: WhatsApp is rolling out a new feature allowing users to communicate using usernames instead of phone numbers, enhancing privacy by avoiding number sharing. The optional feature, expected globally by June 2026, lets users and businesses reserve unique handles. This change addresses growing concerns about digital privacy and data exposure in messaging platforms.

The optional privacy feature is expected to roll out globally by June 2026, with users and businesses able to reserve unique handles.

articles.summary: India's government proposed pre-installing its Aadhaar biometric app on smartphones. Tech giants like Apple and Samsung opposed this move. Companies argue such mandates are problematic and not beneficial for public good.

articles.title: Wipro launches GIFT City Hub to provide services to global BFSI clients
articles.summary: Wipro has opened a new technology hub at GIFT City in Gandhinagar to provide AI-powered services to global banking, financial services, and insurance clients. The facility will initially employ 150 people and aims to support digital banking, capital markets, and regulatory technology solutions. GIFT City is a key initiative under India's financial sector reforms, promoting global business operations from Indian soil.

The hub will support digital banking, capital markets, and regulatory technology solutions.

articles.title: Mobikwik appoints Anis Pathan as chief risk officer
articles.summary: Mobikwik, a digital payments company, has appointed Anis Pathan as its chief risk officer. Pathan will oversee enterprise risk management, including cybersecurity, fraud prevention, and regulatory compliance across the company's UPI, wallet, and merchant payment services. The role underscores the growing focus on governance and risk controls in India's fintech sector, which is regulated by the Reserve Bank of India.
articles.raw_summary: In a statement, the company said Pathan will lead the company’s enterprise risk management (ERM) framework, overseeing strategic, financial, operational, cyber, and fraud risks. His role will also involve strengthening controls across Mobikwik’s core payments businesses, including Unified Payments InterfaceFi (UPI), wallet and merchant payments, as well as building systems for fraud prevention and regulatory compliance.

articles.title: Spice exporters brace for tough times as US buyers stay away
articles.summary: American buyers have stopped contacting Indian spice exporters. This is due to the Iran war. This trade disruption affects India's valuable farm exports. The US is India's largest spice market. This situation could lead to excess domestic supply and lower prices. Exporters and farmers face increased stress. Trade routes are disrupted, and freight costs are high.

articles.title: India's core growth eases to 2.3% in February on poor energy show
articles.summary: India's core sector growth decelerated to a three-month low of 2.3% in February, impacted by subdued electricity generation and declining energy industries. Cement and steel showed growth, driven by government infrastructure initiatives, but overall industrial output is expected to face headwinds from the West Asia crisis.

articles.title: Govt amends norms for exporters availing the interest subvention support
articles.summary: The Indian government has amended rules for exporters receiving interest subvention on credit loans, effective January 2. Exporters will lose benefits if their loans become Non-Performing Assets, and early repayments will only qualify for subvention on the actual outstanding period. Digital processes have been introduced to streamline claims. The changes aim to improve efficiency in trade financing and reduce misuse of government support.
articles.raw_summary: New rules for export credit interest subvention are now in effect from January 2. Exporters will not receive benefits if their loan becomes a Non-Performing Asset. Early loan repayment will only receive subvention for the actual outstanding period. Digital processes are introduced for smoother claims. These changes aim to streamline benefits for trade and banks.

articles.summary: The technology involves ovum pick-up, in vitro fertilization, and embryo transfer. This opens new avenues for rapid genetic improvement of India's dairy breeds.

articles.title: Government amends guidelines for interest subvention support for export credit
articles.summary: The government has amended guidelines for interest subvention support on export credit loans. Under the new rules, interest subvention will no longer be available once a loan account is classified as a non-performing asset (NPA). This change aims to ensure fiscal discipline in export credit financing.
articles.raw_summary: As per the changes, interest subvention shall not be admissible from the date on which the loan account is classified as a non-performing asset (NPA), and no subvention benefit shall be extended with effect from such date.

articles.title: Anti dumping duty suggested on spandex, raw material for foam imported from China, Vietnam
articles.summary: India's Directorate General of Trade Remedies has recommended anti-dumping duties for five years on spandex and flexible slabstock polyol imports. These duties target products from China and Vietnam. The recommendations follow investigations into complaints about dumped goods hurting local manufacturers. The proposed duties aim to protect domestic industries from unfair pricing.

articles.title: New Income Tax Act 2025 to prioritize simplicity and stakeholder engagement, says FM Nirmala Sitharaman
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman announced the Income Tax Act 2025, which will replace the 1961 Act with a simpler framework effective April 1, 2026. The new law aims to improve taxpayer compliance through stakeholder engagement and a collaborative approach. The government will prioritize simplicity and partnership over adversarial tax relationships.

articles.title: Core sector output eased to a three-month low of 2.3% in February
articles.summary: India's core sector growth decelerated to a three-month low of 2.3% in February, impacted by weaker electricity generation and energy industry declines. Cement and steel showed resilience, but refinery products, crude oil, and natural gas contracted. This slowdown signals a broader economic moderation, with forecasts adjusted due to persistent energy price concerns.

articles.title: Centre classifies key energy data as national security; mandates real-time reporting from oil and gas firms
articles.summary: India is now treating vital energy data as a national security matter. All oil and gas companies must share detailed operational information. This move aims to strengthen government oversight and improve responses to supply disruptions. Real-time monitoring of energy supplies will help prioritize critical sectors and make better procurement decisions during crises. This ensures India's energy security.

articles.summary: India's core industries saw slower growth in February, reaching 2.3 percent. This slowdown was mainly due to crude oil and petroleum refinery products. This data reflects the industrial activity across eight key sectors.

articles.title: India’s forex reserves fall $7.05 billion to $709.76 billion as of March 13
articles.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves saw a significant drop. Reserves fell by $7.05 billion to $709.76 billion in the week ending March 13. This follows a previous decline of $11.68 billion. The Reserve Bank of India intervened heavily in currency markets. The central bank sold dollars to support the rupee. Global oil prices and the Iran conflict contributed to this pressure.

articles.summary: A working paper, titled Education and Skilling for Employment: From Credentials to Learning Outcome authored by NITI Aayog member Arvind Virmani, said India’s education and skilling landscape faces challenges such as raising the minimum learning outcomes to the benchmarks appropriate for the per capita GDP levels which we are aiming to reach by 2047 and lowering the drop out rates of students between primary and secondary education.

articles.summary: The Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) has notified new Income Tax rules under the Income-tax Act of 2025, which will take effect from April 1, 2026. The rules enhance House Rent Allowance (HRA) benefits while tightening disclosure requirements and compliance in areas like capital gains and non-resident taxation. Valuation and reporting rules for financial instruments have also been clarified and enhanced.

Meanwhile, Finland retains its title as the happiest nation for the ninth year running, a benchmark many aspire to.

articles.title: Global trade growth to slow to 1.4-1.9%, Middle East conflict a risk: WTO
articles.summary: The World Trade Organization (WTO) has lowered its forecast for global trade growth in 2024 to 1.4-1.9%, down from last year's surge driven by AI-related goods and inventory preloading to avoid tariffs. Ongoing conflicts in the Middle East pose additional risks to trade stability. This slowdown could impact India's export-driven economy and trade policies.

Ongoing tensions in the Middle East could exacerbate these challenges. Last year's growth was markedly influenced by the surge in AI-related goods and a strategic approach to preloading inventory to circumvent tariffs.

articles.title: Farmers seek agri, dairy carve-out in India-US trade deal over duty-free import fears
articles.summary: In a fervent display of solidarity, farmers have rallied against key components of the proposed India-US trade agreement, fearing that duty-free imports could devastate local agriculture. The throngs at Ramlila Maidan echoed their concerns as they insisted on a legal guarantee for the Minimum Support Price (MSP), citing the Swaminathan Commission as a benchmark.

articles.summary: India's trade ministry has recommended imposing anti-dumping duties on a key industrial chemical imported from China. The finance ministry will make the final decision on this matter.

articles.summary: In its report ‘Realising the Export Potential of India’s Sports Equipment Manufacturing Sector’, released on Thursday, the Aayog has proposed a seven-pronged strategy that includes structural reforms and fiscal incentives to the tune of Rs 7500 crore from 2027-2031 to help scale up manufacturing and exports of sports goods and equipment from the country.

articles.summary: India's oilmeal exports to China saw a massive jump this fiscal year. Competitive prices and trade policies favored Indian products. However, this advantage is now challenged. Meanwhile, overall oilmeal exports have declined. Geopolitical conflicts are disrupting shipments to the Middle East and Europe, increasing transit times and costs. This situation threatens India's export competitiveness in key markets.

articles.title: RBI working to provide avenues to Russian trade counterparts for INR use
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is creating new ways for Russian businesses to use their accumulated Indian rupees. Increased transactions will help the market for INR-Rouble trade to grow.

articles.title: Govt launches ₹497-crore RELIEF scheme as West Asia crisis disrupts exports
articles.summary: The Indian government has launched the RELIEF scheme with Rs 497 crore to aid exporters impacted by West Asia conflict disruptions. It also provides partial reimbursement for extraordinary freight and insurance costs for MSME exporters.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu surpasses Gujarat, Maharashtra to become India's top textile exporter
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu has become India's top state for textile exports. Shipments reached USD 7,997.17 million in fiscal year 2024-25. The state's success is attributed to planned government actions and multifaceted growth initiatives.

Bangladesh remains India's largest export market in South Asia.

Compounding these issues were significant delays in merger reporting, which further illuminated the compliance shortcomings.

articles.summary: US Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller expressed concern that a potential US-Israel war with Iran could disrupt global oil supplies by closing the Strait of Hormuz, raising inflation risks. The Strait is a critical chokepoint for oil shipments, and prolonged closure could impact global energy markets. India, heavily reliant on oil imports, may face economic consequences from such a disruption.

articles.title: AI disruption could displace 300 million jobs globally over the next decade: Goldman Sachs
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs estimates that AI could displace 300 million jobs worldwide over the next decade, with most changes happening within 10 years. The investment bank notes this transition will reshape labour markets due to automation and rising demand for technical skills. A modest 0.6 percentage point rise in unemployment is projected, though faster AI adoption could cause larger disruptions.

Goldman’s economists estimate a modest 0.6 percentage point increase in unemployment during this time, noting that a faster wave of adoption could lead to “much larger” economic disruptions.

The move raises concerns about regional stability and could impact global oil supplies, which are vital for India's energy security.

The concerns point to systemic issues that could require policy reforms to address.

articles.title: Mamata Banerjee alleges EC ‘targeting Bengal’, calls it political interference
articles.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee accused the Election Commission of targeting her state by removing over 50 officials ahead of elections, calling it political interference. articles.title: 47% of sitting MLAs in poll-bound West Bengal face criminal cases: ADR
articles.summary: A report by the Association for Democratic Reforms (ADR) reveals that 47% of sitting MLAs in West Bengal face criminal cases, with 37% facing serious charges. The findings come ahead of the state's elections on April 23 and 29, with results declared on May 4. The report also notes that 52% of MLAs are crorepatis and 15% are women. The data raises questions about governance and electoral integrity in the state.
articles.raw_summary: Nearly half of West Bengal's legislators have criminal cases, says ADR. Elections are on April 23 and 29, results on May 4. 47% face charges, 37% serious. 52% are crorepatis, 15% women.

articles.raw_summary: The BJP announced its first list of 144 candidates for the West Bengal Assembly elections. Key candidates are Suvendu Adhikari, Dilip Ghosh, and Swapan Dasgupta.

articles.title: NCP (SP) won't contest against Sunetra Pawar in Baramati assembly bypoll: Sule
articles.summary: NCP (SP) working president Supriya Sule announced that her party will not contest the upcoming Baramati assembly bypoll against Deputy Chief Minister and NCP leader Sunetra Pawar. This decision reflects internal party dynamics and electoral strategy in the bypoll, which is a local-level election to fill a vacant assembly seat.
articles.raw_summary: NCP (SP) working president Supriya Sule on Monday said her party will not field a candidate against Deputy Chief Minister and NCP leader Sunetra Pawar in the upcoming Baramati assembly bypoll.

articles.title: Rajya Sabha Election 2026: What is cross-voting
articles.summary: Cross-voting in Rajya Sabha elections refers to when a Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) votes for a candidate from a different party, contrary to their own party's directive or candidate preferences.
articles.raw_summary: Cross-voting in Rajya Sabha elections refers to when a Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) votes for a candidate from a different party, contrary to their own party's directive or candidate preferences.

articles.title: Fishermen spot PSLV rocket part off Tamil Nadu coast, ISRO confirms debris
articles.summary: Fishermen off the Tamil Nadu coast spotted and recovered debris from an ISRO PSLV rocket. ISRO confirmed the debris originated from one of its rockets, prompting an investigation into its origin and safety implications. The incident raises questions about space debris management and regulatory oversight in India's space activities.
articles.raw_summary: Debris from an ISRO PSLV rocket has been recovered off Tamil Nadu’s coast after fishermen spotted a floating metallic structure, prompting an investigation into its origin and safety implications.

articles.title: Supreme Court to hear plea on Gurugram 4-year-old rape, no arrests yet
articles.summary: The Supreme Court agreed to hear a petition seeking a Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) or Special Investigation Team (SIT) probe into the alleged rape of a 4-year-old child in Gurugram. The petitioner alleged police inaction and no arrests have been made in the case. The court's decision to hear the plea reflects concerns over the handling of such crimes and may prompt further legal or policy action regarding child protection and law enforcement accountability.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court agreed to hear a plea seeking a CBI or SIT probe into the alleged rape of a 4-year-old in Gurugram, with the petitioner alleging police inaction and no arrests so far.

articles.title: Gautam Gambhir files ₹2.5 crore lawsuit over deepfakes: 'My identity has been weaponised'
articles.summary: Gautam Gambhir seeks ₹2.5 crore in damages and a permanent injunction from the Delhi High Court against 16 defendants, including Meta, X Corp., Google, and YouTube, for misuse of his identity.
articles.raw_summary: Gautam Gambhir seeks ₹2.5 crore in damages and a permanent injunction from the Delhi High Court against 16 defendants, including Meta, X Corp., Google, and YouTube, for misuse of his identity.

articles.title: Vadodara car crash: Supreme Court dismisses Gujarat's plea against bail to accused
articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed Gujarat's plea against granting bail to Rakshit Ravish Chorasiya, who was involved in a 2025 Vadodara car crash that killed Hemali Patel. The court considered Chorasiya's age and the non-deliberate nature of the act in its decision. This ruling sets a legal precedent regarding bail conditions in similar cases.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court dismissed Gujarat's plea against bail for Rakshit Ravish Chorasiya, involved in the 2025 Vadodara car crash killing Hemali Patel, noting his age and non-deliberate act.

articles.title: Chhattisgarh passes anti-conversion Bill, repeat offenders to get life term | Key provisions
articles.summary: The Chhattisgarh Assembly passed an anti-conversion bill to prevent forced religious conversions, introducing strict penalties including life imprisonment for repeat offenders and mandatory declarations. The bill was passed despite Congress MLAs boycotting the session. The law aims to regulate religious conversions and has significant legal and governance implications for the state.
articles.raw_summary: The Chhattisgarh Assembly passed a bill to prevent forced religious conversions, with strict penalties and mandatory declarations. Congress MLAs boycotted the session.

articles.title: Israeli Foreign Minister Sa'ar briefs Jaishankar on West Asia conflict
articles.summary: Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar briefed India's External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar about the ongoing West Asia conflict, highlighting Iran's alleged naval terrorism in the Strait of Hormuz. The conflict began on February 28. The discussion underscores India's interest in regional stability, given its energy imports and diplomatic engagements in the Middle East.

articles.title: Maharashtra doubles tax on old vehicles to fight pollution, push cleaner transport
articles.summary: The Maharashtra Assembly has approved a new green tax on old vehicles, doubling rates up to Rs 7,000. The move aims to reduce pollution, generate revenue, and encourage the adoption of cleaner BS-VI and electric vehicles. The tax targets older, more polluting vehicles to push for greener transport options in the state.
articles.raw_summary: Maharashtra Assembly has approved a new green tax on old vehicles, raising rates up to Rs 7,000. The move aims to curb pollution, boost revenue, and push adoption of cleaner BS-VI and electric vehicles.

articles.title: DarkSword spyware puts 270 million iPhones at risk, researchers warn
articles.summary: Researchers have discovered a spyware tool called DarkSword that exploits vulnerabilities in Apple's Safari browser to target iPhones. The malware can expose user data within seconds, putting up to 270 million devices at risk if not updated. Experts warn that this poses a major cybersecurity threat, particularly for users in India where smartphone penetration is high. The incident highlights the need for stronger digital security measures and user awareness.
articles.raw_summary: A newly discovered spyware tool, DarkSword, is targeting iPhones through Safari exploits, exposing data within seconds. Experts warn that up to 270 million devices may be at risk if not updated.

articles.title: DPDP Rules | How to achieve real compliance when intent isn't enough
articles.summary: India's Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Rules have come into force, shifting compliance focus from declarations to verifiable actions. Enterprises now face the challenge of proving compliance rather than just stating intent. The article outlines ten steps to help businesses navigate these new requirements, emphasizing the need for robust documentation and evidence-based adherence to the rules.
articles.raw_summary: With the DPDP Rules now in force, compliance is no longer about what you say—it’s about what you can prove. For enterprises, the real challenge might just have begun. These ten steps can help.

articles.title: Memory chip shortage to continue until 2030, says SK Group chairman
articles.summary: SK Group chairman Chey Tae-won predicts a global shortage of memory chips will persist for the next 4-5 years, with supply unable to meet demand until at least 2030. The shortage is driven by dominant players like SK Hynix, Samsung, and Micron struggling to keep up with demand. This could impact industries reliant on semiconductors, including India's growing electronics and tech sectors. The situation may prompt policy measures to address supply chain vulnerabilities or boost domestic production
articles.raw_summary: Chey Tae-won, SK Group chairman, predicts a global memory chip shortage for 4-5 more years. SK Hynix, Samsung, and Micron control supply but can't meet demand until 2030.

articles.title: Hands-on training must for foreign medical grads: NMC
articles.summary: The National Medical Commission (NMC) has mandated that foreign medical graduates must undergo hands-on training to practice in India.
